metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the chain-rebase API surface: replace `/rebase:chain` with the concrete Gerrit REST call — `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with body `{"rebase_chain": true}` (the `RebaseInput` form) — and add a spike/smoke-test confirming this call on Gerrit 3.14.1 before implementing the chain-rebase path.
findings.finding: The plan writes `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` / `/rebase:chain` using a slash-separator notation that implies `/rebase:chain` is a distinct REST path. In Gerrit's REST API the chain-rebase variant is `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with a `RebaseInput` JSON body containing `{"rebase_chain": true}` — there is no standalone `/rebase:chain` path. The colon-action path form exists in Gerrit (e.g. `/submit`, `/abandon`) but not for the chain-rebase surface.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet: 'POST /changes/{id}/rebase / /rebase:chain'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'POST /changes/{id}/rebase / /rebase:chain' with 'POST /changes/{id}/rebase (body: {"rebase_chain": true} for a relation chain; RebaseInput.rebase_chain, available since Gerrit 3.5, confirmed present in 3.14.1)' and add a one-line smoke-test in the e2e harness that calls this endpoint on a two-change chain.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile parent AC1 'bot-only vote ACL' wording with the design-settled Contributors-votable ACL: update parent AC1 to state 'Contributors-votable ACL (agents set the label under their own identity); bot holds rebase + submit capability only' and update the security invariants section framing in S1 AC5 accordingly.
findings.finding: Parent AC1 states the Autosubmit label has a 'bot-only vote ACL (service account autolander-bot / group Autosubmit Voters)' but every child (S1, S2, S4) consistently implements a Contributors-votable ACL where agents set the label themselves. S1 grants 'label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Contributors'; S2 declares 'the vote is granted to group Contributors, so an agent sets the label'; S4 sets it 'under the agent's OWN Gerrit identity (a Contributor).' The parent's AC1 wording is internally contradicted by the parent's own body ('Autosubmit opt-in label = a lease the agent hands the bot') and by all children. Additionally, parent AC8 lists 'bot-only vote ACL' as a security invariant to enumerate in ADR-0042, so S1 AC5 would document that invariant using the wrong (bot-only) framing — producing a false security claim in the security-invariants artifact.
findings.location: child 843b-801f-9ef9-442d (S1) AC1 / AC5; parent Phase 1 AC1; parent AC8
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite parent AC1 vote-ACL clause to: 'vote ACL granted to Contributors (agents set the label under their own identity via the land command), with the bot holding only rebase-on-behalf-of and submit capabilities; the two-vote gate is enforced by the bot re-verifying is:submittable on the exact tested SHA.' Update S1 AC5 to replace 'bot-only vote ACL' with 'Contributors-votable with bot-only submit capability' in the invariant enumeration.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assign clear ownership: either (a) S6 creates the complete ADR-0042 including the Security invariants section (with S6 depending on S1 for the invariant content), or (b) S1 creates a dedicated security-invariants stub file that S6 references and incorporates — but do NOT split authorship across two stories with no integration dependency.
findings.finding: The ADR-0042 security invariants section content is split across two stories with no integration path: S1 AC5 authors the security invariants content ('the ADR-0042 Security invariants section records...') while S6 creates the ADR-0042 file ('docs/adr/0042-*.md records the auto-lander...') but S6 does NOT depend on S1 and does not mention integrating S1's security invariants content. Neither story assigns ownership of the final, merged ADR-0042 file that contains BOTH the design record (S6's scope) and the security invariants section (S1's scope).
findings.location: child 843b-801f-9ef9-442d (S1) AC5; child b825-a233-97d4-45c2 (S6) AC3; S6 Dependencies
findings.suggested_fix: Add to S6's dependencies: 'Also depends on S1 for the Security invariants section content.' Add to S6 AC3: 'The ADR includes a Security invariants section (content drafted by S1) enumerating each invariant per parent AC8.' Remove the ADR-0042 authorship claim from S1 AC5 and replace it with: 'Document the security invariants in a structured artifact (e.g. infra/gerrit/security-invariants.md or inline in the PR description) that S6 will incorporate into ADR-0042.'

findings.finding: The parent AC specifies a 'bot-only vote ACL (service account `autolander-bot` / group `Autosubmit Voters`)' for the Autosubmit label, but S1 contradicts this by granting the label ACL to group `Contributors` (requesters) so agents can set it under their own identity. These two specifications are irreconcilable as written.
findings.location: child 843b-801f-9ef9-442d: S1 — AC lines 'label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Contributors' vs parent Phase-1 AC bullet 1 'bot-only vote ACL (service account autolander-bot / group Autosubmit Voters)'
findings.suggested_fix: Update the parent AC bullet 1 to read: 'vote ACL granted to Contributors (requesters) so agents may set their own intent; the two-vote gate is not weakened because the bot re-verifies `is:submittable` on the exact tested SHA immediately before submit' — aligning with S1's design rationale and ADR-0042's security invariant argument.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove the 'S9 (batch failure)' consumer reference from S3's Outcome record section; update to list only 'S4 (land command)' as the consumer of the persisted cache in this epic's scope.
findings.finding: S3's outcome record section (and its marker schema description) references 'S9 (batch failure)' as a consumer: 'S4 (land command) and S9 (batch failure) consume this cache + live Gerrit state as the outcome.' S9 is an archived story (the parent explicitly states 'S7-S9 archived') and is absent from the sibling roster. This residual reference introduces a false consumer into the outcome record's interface contract, which could confuse S3's implementer into designing the schema to accommodate a non-existent consumer.
findings.location: child 0525-6d71-8969-4512 (S3), 'Outcome record' section
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'S4 (land command) and S9 (batch failure) consume this cache + live Gerrit state as the outcome' with 'S4 (land command) consumes this cache + live Gerrit state as the outcome (Phase-2 batch-failure handling, formerly S9, is deferred to idea c3a9-5f4c-bdc6-4e27).'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a configurable CI-wait timeout (and polling interval) to the autolander spec: document the default value, make it an environment variable / compose config, and add a timeout-expiry outcome path (e.g. `ci_timeout`) so the bot removes `Autosubmit` and hands back the stack rather than blocking indefinitely.
findings.finding: The serial lander 'awaits a fresh Verified +1' with no configurable timeout or polling-interval specified. If CI hangs (job never completes, no Verified signal arrives), the bot has no defined exit condition, and there is no AC or story note making this threshold configurable or even acknowledged as needing a value.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — serial auto-lander service bullet: 'awaits a fresh Verified +1'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Phase 1 AC, add a bullet: 'CI-wait timeout: configurable via `AUTOLANDER_CI_TIMEOUT_S` (default: e.g. 3600 s); on expiry the bot removes `Autosubmit`, records a `ci_timeout` outcome, and unblocks the wipChain. Polling interval configurable via `AUTOLANDER_POLL_INTERVAL_S` (default: 60 s).' Extend the outcome enum to include `ci_timeout` or subsume it under `ci_failed`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state in the autolander service AC whether it exposes any inbound port (and if so, that it binds to 127.0.0.1 only / is nginx-proxied with the same auth as Gerrit), or confirm it is a purely outbound polling daemon with no listening socket.
findings.finding: The plan specifies the autolander service 'deploys like the review-bot — infra/compose/Dockerfile.* + compose' but does not state whether the container exposes any inbound network port. If it does expose an HTTP/metrics endpoint (e.g. a health-check or status API consumed by `land-status`), the human/admin authentication mechanism for that endpoint is unspecified.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — 'Serial auto-lander service (project infra; deploys like the review-bot — infra/compose/Dockerfile.* + compose)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the failure-handling AC to specify fallback behavior for non-409 REST errors (rebase/submit) and bot crash/restart mid-wipChain, including how the wipChain is released and what outcome (if any) is recorded.
findings.finding: The failure-handling AC specifies recovery for `409` rebase conflicts and CI failures, but provides no fallback for non-409 REST errors during the rebase or submit steps (e.g., HTTP 500, network timeout, auth failure), nor for a bot crash while holding the wipChain. These are incomplete sub-step failure modes the serial lander will encounter in practice.
findings.location: AC Phase 1 — 'Failure handling' bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly document in S3's outcome record section that `change_ids[]` is populated from S2's `chain_member_ids[]` (i.e. all chain members, not just the tip), and align the field name across S2 and S3 or add an explicit mapping note.
findings.finding: S2's wipChain schema uses `chain_member_ids[]` to track the stack members held in memory, while S3's persisted outcome record schema uses `change_ids[]` for the same semantic concept (the ordered list of changes in the stack). S4 reads S3's record (not S2's wipChain) to surface outcomes. These are the same underlying entity (the set of change IDs in the active chain/stack) expressed under different names across the two stories that form the implicit shared state interface. No explicit mapping or aliasing is provided.
findings.location: child 0ee8-08de-bf0f-45ec (S2) AC5 wipChain schema; child 0525-6d71-8969-4512 (S3) Outcome record section
findings.suggested_fix: Add to S3's Outcome record section: 'change_ids[] is populated from S2's wipChain.chain_member_ids[] — all members of the chain, ordered ancestor-first; the tip change is change_id.' Optionally rename one field to match the other across stories.

findings.finding: S6 creates ADR-0042 but its ACs do not include a Security invariants section, leaving Phase-1 invariants (c) 're-verify is:submittable on the exact tested SHA immediately before submit' and (d) 'two-vote gate unweakened' without an owning story to write their concrete checks into ADR-0042. The parent Phase-1 AC explicitly requires all four invariants documented in ADR-0042 with concrete checks (a grep of project.config, a GET /projects/rebar/access diff, and/or a test asserting the re-verify logic). S1 contributes invariants (a) and (b); S8 contributes the Phase-2 SHA-binding invariant. But no story owns writing invariants (c) and (d) — including the concrete test asserting the re-verify logic — into ADR-0042's Security invariants section.
findings.location: child b825-a233-97d4-45c2 (S6) Acceptance Criteria; child 843b-801f-9ef9-442d (S1) Security-invariants AC; parent Phase 1 AC bullet 8 (Security-invariants artifact)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC to S6: 'ADR-0042 includes a Security invariants section covering all four Phase-1 invariants — (a) non-gating Autosubmit, (b) bot-only vote ACL, (c) re-verify is:submittable on exact tested SHA before submit (with a test asserting this in S2's test suite), (d) two-vote gate unweakened — each with its concrete check. S6 assembles this section from S1's and S2's contributions and owns ensuring completeness.'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rename the epic title to remove 'batching' (e.g. 'Parallel-agent auto-lander for main — Phase 1: serial lander') so the title matches the committed scope.
findings.finding: The epic title 'serial->batching' implies batching is delivered in this epic, directly contradicting the body which explicitly defers Phase 2 / cross-stack batching to idea `c3a9-5f4c-bdc6-4e27`. A reader (or executor picking up a child story) could misread scope.
findings.location: Epic title: 'Parallel-agent auto-lander for main — serial->batching (project infrastructure)'
findings.suggested_fix: Rename to: 'Parallel-agent auto-lander for main — serial lander (project infrastructure)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State a default polling interval and maximum timeout for `--wait` in the land command AC (e.g. 'poll every 30 s, time out after the project's CI SLA ceiling, returning `ci_failed` on timeout').
findings.finding: The `--wait` variant of the land command has no specified polling interval or maximum timeout, leaving the executor to 'choose an appropriate' polling cadence and giving no termination guarantee for the blocking call.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria › Phase 1 › Agent landing contract AC: 'blocking (`--wait`) and single-shot status variants'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the AC: 'The `--wait` variant polls every 30 s and exits with `ci_failed` if no terminal outcome is reached within [CI SLA, e.g. 60 min]; the timeout value is configurable via an env var `AUTOLANDER_TIMEOUT_MIN`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC specifying a deterministic in-session test (e.g. a unit test of the lander's state machine using a Gerrit API fake/stub) that asserts the lander does NOT submit until a post-rebase `Verified +1` vote is present on the current patchset.
findings.finding: The AC for the serial lander requires it to 'await a fresh Verified +1' before submitting, but specifies no test or verification method that would prove — within the closing PR's CI — that this temporal/state-machine behavior is actually enforced. Without a specified test, the criterion is unverifiable in-session.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria › Phase 1 › Serial auto-lander service AC: 'rebases to the current tip … → awaits a fresh `Verified +1` → ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the 'bot is the single source of truth' claim (AC item 5) with the Context's split provenance model: either restate the AC to say 'the land command is the single interface (bot-state for `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed`; Gerrit-native for `merged`/`review_failed`)' or define the bot as a thin pass-through that always records all four outcomes so the AC's claim is literally true.
findings.finding: The Context's outcome-provenance note states that `merged` and `review_failed` outcomes are 'derived from native Gerrit status / an `LLM-Review -1` by the land command (S4)', meaning the land command does direct Gerrit correlation for those two outcomes — yet AC item 5 asserts 'The bot is the single source of truth for the outcome.' These two statements are in tension: if the land command independently derives `merged`/`review_failed` from Gerrit primitives, then Gerrit (not the bot) is the truth-source for those outcomes.
findings.location: Context — 'Outcome provenance' note vs. AC Phase 1 item 5 ('Agent landing contract')
findings.suggested_fix: Revise AC item 5 to read: 'The land command is the single interface the agent uses for outcomes; it reads bot-recorded state for `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed` and Gerrit-native status / `LLM-Review` votes for `merged`/`review_failed` — the agent never correlates Gerrit primitives directly.' This aligns the AC with the Context's provenance model without overstating 'single source of truth.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC item (or extend AC item 5) that requires the outcome-record schema to be defined (field names, types, required fields) and stored in a versioned artifact in `infra/` — and that the bot's state-persistence logic is covered by at least one integration test asserting round-trip correctness for each bot-recorded outcome (`needs_rebase`, `ci_failed`).
findings.finding: The Greenfield note explicitly lists 'the outcome-record schema' and 'the autolander service + its `wipChain` and state dir' as new artifacts created by this epic, and the Context references the bot recording state to S3 — but no AC item covers the specification, format, or verifiability of the outcome-record schema or the state directory structure. These artifacts are named but have no covering criterion.
findings.location: Greenfield note ('the outcome-record schema', 'state dir'); Context ('the bot *records* `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed` in its own state (S3)'); AC Phase 1 — no item covers schema

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out procedure for the `refs/meta/config` push (e.g., 'revert to the pre-cutover snapshot commit on refs/meta/config via `git push origin <snapshot-sha>:refs/meta/config`') to the ADR-0042 Security invariants section or a dedicated rollout AC.
findings.finding: The plan specifies pushing a new `Autosubmit` label to `refs/meta/config` (a shared, authoritative Gerrit config branch) but provides no rollback or back-out plan if the config push is malformed or introduces unintended ACL effects.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria > Phase 1 > first bullet: '`Autosubmit` Gerrit label authored in `infra/gerrit/project.config` / pushed to `refs/meta/config`'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Phase 1 AC (or ADR-0042), add a step: 'Before pushing, tag the current refs/meta/config HEAD as `autolander-pre-cutover`; rollback procedure is `git push origin autolander-pre-cutover:refs/meta/config --force` (Gerrit admins only). Verify the rollback restores the pre-cutover GET /projects/rebar/access snapshot.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly assign the S3 state schema (field names, retention policy) and `wipChain` lifecycle (how to inspect and reset a stuck entry) to a documentation deliverable — either ADR-0042 or a dedicated ops runbook in `infra/`.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new `wipChain` state directory in S3 and a new outcome-record schema as first-class operational artifacts, but does not specify documentation for their schema, lifecycle, or operational debugging — leaving maintainers without a reference when the bot behaves unexpectedly.
findings.location: Context section: 'the autolander service + its `wipChain` and state dir'; Acceptance Criteria > Phase 1 > 'Outcome provenance: the bot records `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed` in its own state (S3)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the serial lander AC that the bot awaits a fresh `Verified +1` on **every change in the relation chain** (not just the tip) before attempting submit, and that any per-change CI failure causes the **whole stack** to be handed back (matching the 'never partial-land' invariant).
findings.finding: The 'ancestor-atomic submit' mechanism is underspecified for multi-change stacks: the plan says the lander 'awaits a fresh Verified +1' and then 'ancestor-atomic submits,' but does not specify whether it awaits Verified on ALL changes in the relation chain (not just the tip) before attempting submit. Under Gerrit FFO + relation-chain semantics, submitting the tip change only proceeds atomically if every ancestor in the chain also satisfies all submit requirements (LLM-Review=MAX, Verified=MAX, no unresolved). If a mid-chain change has a Verified -1 (e.g. CI failed on its own patchset after a rebase), the lander must detect this and hand back the whole stack — but the plan's 'awaits a fresh Verified +1' language implies it only watches the front change. This is a hidden ordering assumption that breaks the 'never partial-land' invariant when the chain has more than one change.
findings.location: AC: Serial auto-lander service bullet — 'awaits a fresh Verified +1 → ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA'; Context — 'The stack is the atomic unit for landing AND failure: never partial-land'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend the serial lander service AC bullet to read: '…rebase the full chain to the current tip (POST /changes/{tip}/rebase:chain, on_behalf_of_uploader) → await a fresh Verified +1 on **each change in the chain** → ancestor-atomic submit of the exact tested tip SHA.' Add a corresponding failure-handling bullet: 'if any change in the chain receives Verified -1 after the chain rebase, remove Autosubmit from all chain changes and hand the whole stack back.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` and the `onBehalfOfUploader` parameter are available and ACL-correct on Gerrit 3.14.1 (cite the Gerrit 3.14 REST API docs or add a smoke-test call to `infra/gerrit/smoke-check.sh`); document any required service-account permission grants alongside the `Autosubmit Voters` group setup.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` with `on_behalf_of_uploader` as the chain-rebase mechanism, but neither capability is verified against the installed Gerrit 3.14.1 — a version where the project has a documented history of catching API quirks (e.g., `NoBlock` required on 3.14.1). The `rebase:chain` endpoint and `onBehalfOfUploader` rebase parameter both exist in modern Gerrit, but the plan provides no explicit confirmation (a test call, a Gerrit changelog reference, or a docs citation) that these work as specified under 3.14.1. If `on_behalf_of_uploader` has a different permission model or parameter name in 3.14.1, the bot will either fail to rebase or will mint patchsets under the bot's identity — silently stripping DCO trailers and changing the committer recorded on `main`.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet: 'rebases to the current tip (`POST /changes/{id}/rebase` / `/rebase:chain`, `on_behalf_of_uploader` to preserve author/DCO/`rebar-ticket` trailers)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `smoke-check.sh` step (or a note in the ADR-0042 Security invariants section) that calls `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with `{"onBehalfOfUploader": true}` on a test change and verifies the resulting patchset's author matches the original uploader. Reference the Gerrit 3.14 release notes (https://www.gerritcodereview.com/3.14.html) to confirm `rebase:chain` availability.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document whether the GerritForge `submit-queue` plugin (or equivalent hosted Gerrit commit-queue tooling) was evaluated and why a bespoke service was preferred (e.g., plugin doesn't support the `on_behalf_of_uploader` rebase, requires a different Gerrit version, adds a `.jar` dependency outside the project's plugin-pinning discipline, or lacks the typed-outcome agent contract the plan requires).
findings.finding: The plan builds a bespoke serial lander service but does not evaluate the GerritForge `submit-queue` plugin — a maintained upstream Gerrit plugin that implements exactly the same serial rebase-await-CI-submit loop the plan describes. ADR-0040 dismissed Zuul (correct — wrong cost/complexity tier), but the submit-queue plugin is a much closer match in scope. Without documenting why it was not adopted, the plan risks reinventing a maintained upstream artifact.
findings.location: Context — Design settled section; Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an `aws_s3_bucket` (+ `prevent_destroy`, encryption, versioning) for autolander outcome state and a scoped `aws_iam_role_policy` granting only `s3:PutObject`/`s3:GetObject`/`s3:ListBucket` on that bucket's ARN to the existing `rebar-gerrit-instance-role`.
findings.finding: The plan stores autolander bot outcome state in S3 ('the bot records needs_rebase/ci_failed in its own state (S3)') but no IaC is specified for this S3 bucket — no aws_s3_bucket resource, no prevent_destroy lifecycle, no versioning/encryption settings, and no scoped IAM policy granting the autolander-bot's instance role s3:PutObject/GetObject on that bucket.
findings.location: Context section — 'Outcome provenance: the bot records needs_rebase/ci_failed in its own state (S3)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to infra/terraform/ a new file (e.g. autolander.tf) declaring: aws_s3_bucket.autolander_state with server-side encryption and lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }; aws_iam_role_policy.autolander_s3 attaching a policy document scoped to arn:aws:s3:::${bucket}/* with only the needed s3: actions.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `/rebar/prod/autolander-bot-token` (and any GitHub PAT if ticket ops are needed) to `ssm.tf`'s `rebar_secret_params` list, and document its fetch in `fetch-secrets.sh`.
findings.finding: The autolander-bot service account requires Gerrit credentials (and possibly a GitHub PAT for ticket ops via `import rebar`), but no new SSM SecureString parameter slot for these credentials is specified — the existing `ssm.tf` rebar_secret_params list does not include any autolander-bot token.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — 'bot-only vote ACL (service account `autolander-bot` / group `Autosubmit Voters`)'; Context — 'it may import rebar for ticket ops'
findings.suggested_fix: Append '"/rebar/prod/autolander-bot-token"' to the rebar_secret_params list in infra/terraform/ssm.tf and add the corresponding mapping in infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh so the container .env receives AUTOLANDER_BOT_TOKEN on boot.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a CloudWatch alarm (e.g. `monitoring_autolander.tf`) for autolander service health — mirroring the review-bot pattern — wired to the existing `rebar-gerrit-alerts` SNS topic.
findings.finding: The plan adds a new long-running autolander service but specifies no CloudWatch alarm for its liveness/health, despite the established per-service monitoring pattern (monitoring_s4b.tf for review-bot, monitoring_s5.tf for replication, monitoring.tf for Gerrit gate-down) that the plan implicitly inherits.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — 'Observability: the bot logs to its own service logs (container stdout → existing CloudWatch monitoring)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf with an aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm watching a heartbeat metric published by the autolander (e.g. Rebar/Autolander:Heartbeat, treat_missing_data=breaching), following the monitoring_s4b.tf pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify `on_behalf_of_uploader` works on this Gerrit 3.14.1 instance by (a) confirming the `rebaseOnBehalfOf` global capability exists on 3.14.1, (b) adding a grant for `autolander-bot` (or the `Autosubmit Voters` group) in `setup-project.sh` / global capabilities, and (c) adding a smoke-test in the e2e harness that performs an `on_behalf_of_uploader` rebase and asserts the resulting patchset retains the original author and DCO trailer.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `on_behalf_of_uploader` (a `RebaseInput` field) to preserve DCO Signed-off-by / `rebar-ticket` trailers after a bot-initiated rebase, but does not state the required Gerrit permission. Using `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` requires the rebasing account (autolander-bot) to hold the `rebaseOnBehalfOf` capability in Gerrit's global capabilities or equivalent project-level ACL, or the call returns a 403. This permission surface is entirely absent from the plan and not wired in `infra/gerrit/project.config` or `setup-project.sh`. The codebase has no existing usage of this field to validate it works on this Gerrit 3.14.1 instance.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet: 'on_behalf_of_uploader to preserve author/DCO/rebar-ticket trailers'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state the auth preconditions for `autolander-bot`: name the SSM parameter path for its HTTP token, reference the `service-user.sh`-style provisioning step that creates the account and adds it to `Contributors` + `Autosubmit Voters`, and confirm whether REST calls use HTTP basic auth (same as the existing bots) or SSH.
findings.finding: The plan does not state the auth/credential preconditions for the `autolander-bot` service account's Gerrit REST API access: no SSM parameter name, no `.env` key, no `setup-project.sh` / `service-user.sh` analogue for the bot account creation, and no statement of whether it uses HTTP credentials or SSH. The only reference is 'deploys like the review-bot', which is an analogy, not a specification. Given that `requireSignedOffBy` and the Contributors submit ACL make credential correctness critical (a mis-credentialed bot submitting as admin would bypass the Contributors ACL check), this is a concrete gap.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet and Security-invariants AC

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the secret-injection mechanism for the `autolander-bot` service-account credential (e.g. Docker secrets, a named secrets manager, or an injected env-var from a vault) in the AC or ADR-0042 Security invariants — credential handling for a submit-to-main service account must not be left unspecified.
findings.finding: The plan is silent on how the `autolander-bot` service-account credential (the token/password that authorises Gerrit REST calls including submits to `main`) is injected into the container at runtime — there is no declaration of a secrets manager, environment variable injection policy, or equivalent. A bot holding submit-to-`main` rights whose secret lifecycle is unspecified risks the credential being baked into a Dockerfile, committed to a compose file, or logged on startup.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — 'Serial auto-lander service (project infra; deploys like the review-bot — `infra/compose/Dockerfile.*` + compose)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC bullet or ADR-0042 Security invariants entry: 'The `autolander-bot` credential is injected at runtime via [Docker secrets / AWS Secrets Manager / equivalent]; it MUST NOT appear in any Dockerfile, compose file, or container log. The compose service definition uses `secrets:` or an external env-var populated by CI/CD — never a hardcoded value.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the scope of the Gerrit `Run As` / `on_behalf_of_uploader` ACL grant for `autolander-bot` in ADR-0042 Security invariants — specify the minimal required permission (e.g. scoped to non-owner rebase only, not project-wide `Run As`) and add a concrete verifiable check (a `grep` of `project.config` or `GET /projects/rebar/access` diff) confirming it.
findings.finding: The plan requires the autolander to call Gerrit's rebase API with `on_behalf_of_uploader`, which requires the `autolander-bot` service account to hold Gerrit `Run As` (impersonation) rights. The plan never declares the scope of this impersonation grant — specifically, whether the bot can impersonate any Gerrit user project-wide or only act on changes where `Autosubmit` is set by the uploader. An over-scoped `Run As` ACL is a privilege-escalation surface: the bot could be used (or exploited) to perform actions as any user.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — 'rebases to the current tip … `on_behalf_of_uploader` to preserve author/DCO/`rebar-ticket` trailers'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the outcome-record schema (field names, types, required/optional) that the autolander bot writes to S3 and the land command reads, co-locating it with the land-command AC or in a dedicated schema section.
findings.finding: The 'outcome-record schema' is listed as a new artifact in the Greenfield note and referenced in the Context ('the bot records needs_rebase/ci_failed in its own state (S3)'), but no field vocabulary is defined anywhere in the plan — not field names, types, required vs optional, or versioning. An implementer of the land command (S4) that must read this record to surface ci_failed/needs_rebase outcomes has no schema to program against.
findings.location: Context section — 'Outcome provenance' parenthetical; Greenfield note: 'the outcome-record schema … [is a] NEW artifact'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a schema block to the land-command AC (or a new 'State & Schema' section): e.g., `{ change_id: str, outcome: 'needs_rebase'|'ci_failed', recorded_at: ISO8601, patchset: int, detail: str|null }`. Note which fields are written by the bot vs. enriched by the land command.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `wipChain` structure, storage location (e.g., in-process asyncio lock + persistent S3 key for crash-recovery), and the acquire/release protocol so serial-lander implementers have a concrete contract.
findings.finding: The `wipChain` is named as a new artifact with a 'serialized, one instance' constraint in the serial-lander AC, but its structure, placement (in-memory queue? on-disk file? S3 key?), and acquisition/release protocol are never specified. Any story implementing the serial lander needs to know how to acquire, update, and release the wipChain without a race.
findings.location: AC — Phase 1, serial lander bullet: 'Single `wipChain` (serialized, one instance)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Duplicate or cross-reference the outcome-provenance rule (bot-recorded vs. land-command-derived outcomes) directly in the land-command AC bullet so an executor working from the AC alone sees the full protocol.
findings.finding: The outcome-provenance rule (which outcomes the bot *records* vs. which the land command *derives* live from Gerrit) is defined only in a parenthetical in the Context section, not co-located with the land-command AC where an implementer will work. This is an instruction-locality gap: an executor implementing the land command from the AC alone will not know that `merged` and `review_failed` must be derived from Gerrit state at call time, not read from the bot's S3 record.
findings.location: Context section — 'Outcome provenance' parenthetical; AC Phase 1 — 'Agent landing contract' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the land-command AC bullet: '(Source: `ci_failed`/`needs_rebase` are read from bot's S3 state record; `merged`/`review_failed` are derived live from Gerrit change status / `LLM-Review` label at poll time — see Context: Outcome provenance.)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to S1 AC5 (or S6 AC3) an explicit requirement to include a 'GET /projects/rebar/access diff vs the pre-cutover snapshot' output as the per-invariant proof for the vote-ACL invariant in the ADR-0042 Security invariants section.
findings.finding: Parent AC8 requires the security-invariants artifact to include 'a GET /projects/rebar/access diff vs the pre-cutover snapshot' as one of the per-invariant concrete checks proving invariants hold. No child's acceptance criteria include this check. S1 AC5 covers only the grep-of-project.config check and the re-verify-at-submit check; neither S1 nor S6 mentions the GET /projects/rebar/access comparison. This specific proof artifact is uncovered by the union of children.
findings.location: child 843b-801f-9ef9-442d (S1) AC5; child b825-a233-97d4-45c2 (S6) AC3; parent Phase 1 AC8
findings.suggested_fix: Append to S1 AC5: 'and a GET /projects/rebar/access diff vs the pre-cutover snapshot proving the vote ACL matches the config-declared group.'

findings.finding: The Phase 1 security-invariants AC requires 'a `GET /projects/rebar/access` diff vs the pre-cutover snapshot' as proof of the gate-unchanged invariant, but no AC or described work covers *creating and storing* that pre-cutover snapshot before cutover occurs. The verification step presupposes an artifact with no criterion for its production.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC → 'Security-invariants artifact' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the security-invariants AC: 'Before pushing the `Autosubmit` label config, capture `GET /projects/rebar/access` output and commit it as `infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json`; the post-cutover diff is computed against this file in CI.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
